catch.def: 抓住、趕上、領會、接住、隱陷阱、吸引人的事物、接住的人的人物、抓住地 (抓)
catch.col: catch a cold, catch the bus, what's the catch? (有什麼陷阱), eye-catching (奪目)
catch.ex: I had to run fast to catch the last train home.

catcher.ex: The baseball catcher caught the fast ball with great skill.

catching.def: 具傳染性的、迷人的的人 (或物) (迷)
catching.ex: Her cheerful laughter is truly catching for everyone around.

catchment.def: 集水、集水區的人物 (單數)
catchment.ex: The mountain valley acts as a natural catchment area for rain.

catchup/ketchup.ex: He loves to have some tomato catchup with his salty fries.

catch-up/catchup.def: 趕上、追補、趕上的人 (或物) (趕)
catch-up/catchup.ex: The team had to play catch-up after a poor first half.

catchword.def: 標語、口號、檢索詞的人的人物 (單數)
catchword.ex: 'Change' became a popular catchword during the long election.

catchy.def: (曲)朗朗上口的、動聽的、狡猾的人人物 (好)
catchy.trans: catch (名詞/動詞/助動詞/形容詞/副詞)
catchy.ex: The new commercial has a very catchy tune that sticks in your mind.

catechism.def: 問答集、教義問答人物 (單數)
catechism.ex: The children had to memorize the shorter religious catechism.

catechist.def: 傳教員、問答教學者 (單數)
catechist.ex: The dedicated catechist spent years teaching in the local mission.

catechize.def: 用問答方式教學、訊問的人的人物、教學地 (教)
catechize.ex: The lawyer began to catechize the witnesses about the night in question.

categorical.def: 絕對的、明確的、類別的的人物
categorical.ex: The government issued a categorical denial of the rumors.

categorically.ex: She stated categorically that she would not resign today.

categorization.ex: The systematic categorization of the library books took three months.

categorize.def: 分類、歸類、分類的人的人、分類地 (分)
categorize.ex: It's difficult to categorize his music into just one genre.

category.def: 種類、類別、屬的人人物 (單數)
category.ex: The entries are divided into three broad categories of age.

cater.def: 承辦伙食、迎合、投合的人的人、服務地 (服)
cater.ex: The local hotel will cater for the large wedding reception.

caterer.def: 配餐者、承辦伙食者 (單數)
caterer.ex: They hired a professional wedding caterer for the special day.

catering.def: 飲食業、承辦伙食的人 (或物) (食)
catering.ex: My first job was working in a catering service at the airport.

caterpillar.def: 毛蟲、(中)拖拉機的人物 (單數)
caterpillar.ex: A fuzzy green caterpillar was crawling along the leaf edge.

caterwaul.def: (貓)嗥叫、尖叫的人的人物、叫地 (叫)
caterwaul.ex: The loud caterwaul of the neighbor's cat woke me up.

catgut.def: 羊腸弦(樂器、手術用) (單數)
catgut.ex: Strong catgut was used in the past to string tennis rackets.

catharsis.def: (劇)宣洩、(心)淨化、導瀉的人物 (單數)
catharsis.ex: For many, writing poetry is a form of emotional catharsis.

cathartic.def: 宣洩的、導瀉的、瀉藥的人的人物 (瀉)
cathartic.ex: Sharing her story was a very cathartic experience for her today.

cathedral.def: 大教堂、大教堂的的人物 (單數)
cathedral.ex: St. Paul's Cathedral is a famous landmark in central London.

catheter.ex: The patient had to have a urinary catheter fitted after the surgery.

cathode.ex: Early televisions used a heavy cathode ray tube to show images.

catholic.def: 天主教、天主教徒、寬宏大量的、普遍的人的人物 (廣)
catholic.col: catholic church, catholic tastes (多元品味)
catholic.ex: He is a man of very catholic tastes in music and fine art.

catholicize.def: 使受天主教影響、使普遍化的人的人、影響地 (化)
catholicize.ex: The missionaries sought to catholicize the local region peacefully.

catkin.def: (植)葇荑花序、柳絮的人物 (單數)
catkin.ex: The willow catkins began to appear in the early spring heat.

catnap.def: 打盹、小睡、打盹的人的人物、小睡地 (盹)
catnap.ex: I like to take a quick catnap on the sofa after lunch.

catnip.ex: The cat went crazy after smelling the fresh green catnip.

cat's-paw/catspaw.def: 傀儡、被人利用者、微風的人物 (單數)
cat's-paw/catspaw.ex: He realized he was being used as a political cat's-paw for the party.

catsup/ketchup.ex: The children put lots of sweet catsup on their golden fries.
